steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the "overall structure" of the text — meaning what the text does first and what it does second, not what it argues or evaluates.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'lifelong interest in folk music ... debates whether pop influenced "Jolene"': The passage never debates pop's influence on "Jolene" — it says folk ballads influenced that song. ✗
  • Choice B — 'discusses a particular recording period, then considers an earlier period': The text opens on the late 1970s – early 1980s period ("Here You Come Again"), then the phrase "A decade earlier, however" pivots to her earlier folk roots ("Jolene"). ✓
  • Choice C — 'enthusiastically recommends ... expresses disappointment': The text is descriptive, not evaluative. No recommendation or disappointment appears. ✗
  • Choice D — 'praises "Here You Come Again," then notes how similar it is to an earlier song': The text never compares the two songs to each other. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
